meta.title: Corrosive makeup shift
meta.subtitle: Softer, low-alkalinity makeup water turns the loop corrosive
meta.provenance_page: All series simulated; see per-series provenance.

series_provenance: synthetic scenario generator (seeded) → TGF physics engine

indices.provenance: tgf_dosing physics engine (LSI, RSI via vendored cooling-tower-chem formulas); PSI from the Puckorius equilibrium pH

anomalies.note: pH=7.50, z-score=-4.24 (mean=7.74, σ=0.06); pH has been anomalous for 3 consecutive cycles

anomalies_provenance: tgf_dosing statistical anomaly detector (z-score + rate-of-change + persistence; MOMENT not used)

rationale: LSI has gone negative (-0.27) and RSI is 8.0 — the water turned corrosive after the makeup shift.
Low-alkalinity makeup water reduced the buffering and the protective saturation margin.
Raising the scale/corrosion inhibitor to 13.6 mL/min restores the protective film.
recommendation.projected: inhibitor residual held near 12 ppm across the horizon (simulated)

safety_checks.name: Dose rate limit
safety_checks.detail: ≤ 30% step change and ≤ 10 kg/hr pump limit

safety_checks.name: Daily cumulative cap
safety_checks.detail: 46.94 kg dosed in last 24 h vs 240 kg cap

safety_checks.name: Discharge interlock (CoC / CPCB pH)
safety_checks.detail: CoC 3.6 within 8 limit; discharge pH 7.4 within CPCB range

recommendation.provenance: tgf_dosing MPC optimizer + safety layer, run offline; trajectories from the TGF simulator. Closed-loop is backtest-only.

alerts.text: Anomaly on ph — pH=7.50, z-score=-4.24 (mean=7.74, σ=0.06); pH has been anomalous for 3 consecutive cycles
Saturation index turned negative — corrosive tendency
